failure_surface: Excel workbook corruption during save operation, causing ValueError when attempting to reload the modified file
root_cause: Agent created a new empty workbook using openpyxl.Workbook() instead of loading and modifying the existing workbook, which destroyed the original file structure and produced an invalid Excel file
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Always load the existing workbook with load_workbook() before modification, make changes to the loaded object, and save it back. Avoid recreating workbooks from scratch unless explicitly required.
evidence: File copy was successful but subsequent load_workbook(output_path) failed with 'Unable to read workbook... invalid XML'
Code shows wb_new = Workbook() creating blank workbook, then copying data sheet by sheet via openpyxl
This approach loses formulas, merged cells, formatting, and internal structure that openpyxl cannot fully replicate when manually writing cells
First error also shows TypeError from unhandled mixed data types (str/int) in WHS column
SyntaxError shows invalid nested ternary expression in cell_value assignment

memory_items.title: Preserve Existing Workbook Structure
memory_items.lesson: When modifying Excel files, always load the existing workbook first with load_workbook() and edit it in-place rather than creating a new Workbook() instance from scratch.
memory_items.generalization: Recreating a workbook from scratch using Workbook() destroys formulas, styles, merged cells, and hidden content. Load-modify-save pattern preserves these elements.

memory_items.title: Handle Mixed Data Types During Read Operations
memory_items.lesson: Before performing operations like sorting or filtering on DataFrame columns, ensure uniform data types by converting heterogeneous values appropriately.
memory_items.generalization: Columns may contain mixed types (string, int, float). Use astype(str) or pd.to_numeric with errors='coerce' before comparisons to avoid TypeError exceptions.

memory_items.title: Validate Syntax Before Execution
memory_items.lesson: Complex nested conditional expressions (nested ternaries) are prone to syntax errors. Break them into clearer multi-line conditionals or use standard if-elif-else blocks.
memory_items.generalization: Python ternary operators chain poorly for complex logic. Prefer explicit conditional statements for readability and error avoidance.

edits.content:
**Workbook Modification Pattern**: When modifying existing workbooks, always use `load_workbook()` to open the file, make edits to the loaded object, then call `.save()` on the same object. Never recreate workbooks from `Workbook()` class unless you intend to build a completely new file. This preserves formulas, styles, merged cells, and internal XML structure that manual cell writing cannot replicate.

**Data Type Consistency**: Before sorting, filtering, or comparing values in DataFrame columns, verify type homogeneity. Convert mixed-type columns to string with `.astype(str)` or use `pd.to_numeric(errors='coerce')` to prevent TypeErrors.

**Conditional Expression Safety**: Avoid deeply nested ternary expressions (e.g., `a if b if c else d else e`). Use explicit if-elif-else blocks for clarity and to reduce syntax errors.
